page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m1.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ecs.u2i family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance features 16 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 16 vCPUs. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per core.

This server operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include any local storage, meaning external block storage must be attached. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with the instance.

With a 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to memory, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but have minimal memory footprints. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention. It is suitable for workloads such as batch processing, compute-intensive simulations, and lightweight web serving or CI/CD tasks that do not demand large memory capacities.

Powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon processor, it provides 16 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, totaling 16 vCPUs. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1.0 GB per core memory allocation. It runs on the KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. This hardware profile is optimized for compute-intensive tasks that require dedicated CPU resources with low memory overhead. It is suitable for batch processing, distributed computing nodes, and lightweight web serving.
